LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING TO SUSTAIN THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND ON ALL RESPECTS BUT ONE: THE COURT IS ALLOWING LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE A VIOLATION OF BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 828; PLAINTIFF IS TO IDENTIFY WHAT RECORDS ARE REQUIRED TO BE EXCLUDED BUT WERE NOT. PLAINTIFF HAS 30 DAYS FROM TODAY TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. COUNSEL FOR THE BOARD IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A SEPARATE ORDER THAT DR. GOODRICH WAS NEVER ON PROBATION. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-03-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Continued To May-22-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ian E. Goldenberg, Esq., Telephone 415-741-1798, appearing for Plaintiff Karen Goodman, M.D. who is also present;
Mary Cain-Simon, Esq., Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General, Telephone 415-510-3884 for Defendant Medical Board of California;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Medical Board of California's demurrer to Plaintiff's second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend on the grounds that it fails to state facts sufficient to constitute any cause of action. (Code Civ. Proc. 340.) The Court sustained the Medical Board's previous demurrers in part without leave to amend, but granted Plaintiff leave to amend her claim that the Medical Board has violated Business and Professions Code Section 828 by not excluding investigative reports from information provided to persons outside of the Medical Board. Plaintiff's second amended complaint does not allege any facts showing any breach of confidentiality of the records contained within the Medical Board's investigative files, nor has she alleged which such records were supposedly disclosed or provided by the Medical Board, when, or to whom. Nor has Plaintiff met her burden to demonstrate how the complaint can be amended to state a valid cause of action.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported.=(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Continued To Mar-27-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-28-2018 Continued To Dec-19-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2018 Continued To Oct-17-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ian Goldenberg, Esq. 415-741-1798 for Plaintiff Karen Goodrich.
Mary Cain-Simon, Deputy Attorney General 415-510-3884 of the Office of the Attorney General for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues violations of the business and professions code 803.1(d), 828 and 805. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Plaintiff may submit a proposed order for the Court's signature that Dr. Goodrich was never on probation.

DEFENDANT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling to sustain the Demurrer without leave to amend on all respects but one: the Court is allowing leave to amend to allege a violation of business and professions code section 828; Plaintiff is to identify what records are required to be excluded but were not. Plaintiff has 30 days from today to file a second amended Complaint.

Counsel for the Board is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Plaintiff to prepare a separate order that Dr. Goodrich was never on probation. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Continued To Aug-29-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Mary Cain-Simon, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, T:415.510.3884, for defendant/moving party;
Ian E. Goldenberg, Esq., T:415.741.1798, for plaintiff.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court modifies the tentative ruling, as follows:

DEFENDANT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend, for reasons stated on the record.

Counsel for the Medical Board is to prepare a proposed order and submit it in compliance with CRC. 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
